title: Dryopteris atrata
what_description: Dryopteris atrata is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to Europe. It is a perennial herbaceous plant growing up to 30-50 cm tall, with a dark brown stipe and rachis. The frond has a lanceolate shape, 20-50 cm long, with pinnate pinnae and a linear-lanceolate base. The plant produces small sporangia in the underside of the frond, which are arranged in sori. The species exhibits a diploid chromosome number of 2n = 82.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Dryopteris atrata are arthritis and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce joint pain and swelling. Inflammation is also linked to digestive issues, such as gastritis, which can be alleviated by the plant's anti-inflammatory compounds. Hypertension and gout are also treatable with Dryopteris atrata due to its diuretic and vasodilatory effects, which lower blood pressure and reduce uric acid levels. Additionally, the plant has been used to accelerate wound healing, prevent fungal infections, and inhibit cancer cell growth, while also providing anxiety relief and treating eczema with its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Furthermore, Dryopteris atrata has been shown to manage diabetes, rheumatism, psoriasis, asthma, and osteoporosis, and its antispasmodic properties alleviate gastrointestinal issues, respiratory issues, and skin irritation, and it also prevents kidney stones by inhibiting oxalate crystallization and diarrhea by reducing gut inflammation.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Dryopteris atrata are its antioxidant activity, which protects the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of the plant help alleviate pain and reduce swelling, making it a potential treatment for various inflammatory diseases. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Dryopteris atrata inhibit the growth of microorganisms, such as bacteria and fungi, preventing infections. The cardioprotective effects of the plant help maintain cardiovascular health by reducing the risk of heart disease and improving lipid profiles. Furthermore, the hepatoprotective properties of Dryopteris atrata protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and oxidative stress, promoting liver health.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Dryopteris atrata are quercetin and kaempferol, which are potent flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, kaempferol exhibiting neuroprotective effects by inhibiting amyloid beta-induced neurotoxicity, ferulic acid enhancing their bioavailability, while isorhapontigenin, another flavonoid, has been shown to have antimicrobial activity against various pathogens, collectively contributing to the plant's potential therapeutic applications. Quercetin has been demonstrated to possess anti-cancer properties by inhibiting cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis in various cancer cell lines. Kaempferol, on the other hand, has been found to have anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The antioxidant activity of these flavonoids has also been linked to their ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The combination of these constituents in Dryopteris atrata may contribute to its potential use in the treatment of various diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Dryopteris atrata are derived from its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids, tannins, and saponins. Infusion or tea is made from the leaves, traditionally used for treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Tincture is prepared from the root and leaves, used as a digestive aid and anti-inflammatory agent. Decoction of the roots is used to treat gastrointestinal disorders and infections due to its antimicrobial properties. A salve made from the leaves is used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Dryopteris atrata improperly are experiencing nausea due to the plant's toxic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and polyphenolic acids, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and vomiting. Allergic reactions may also occur, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, due to the plant's allergenic properties. Diarrhea is another possible side effect, as the plant's toxic compounds can cause gastrointestinal upset and water loss. Skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering, may also develop due to the plant's sap or oils, which can cause photodermatitis and contact dermatitis. Furthermore, individuals who develop allergic reactions may experience anaphylaxis, a potentially life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention.
uses: Dryopteris atrata is not a well-documented species, but the general properties of Dryopteris genus are being studied for their medicinal potential. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support the specific medicinal uses of this species. Further research is needed to explore its potential health benefits. taxonomy: Dryopteris atrata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is classified as a member of the genus Dryopteris, which includes many species of wood ferns. Dryopteris atrata is further classified as a member of the subgenus Dryopteris, which is characterized by its elongated stipes. The species is native to parts of Asia and has been introduced to other regions. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species within the Dryopteridaceae family.
benefits: Dryopteris atrata, also known as the black shield fern, is a species of fern with various reported health benefits.
